text.title: refactoring-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for refactoring-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Big-bang refactoring — changing everything at once across many files
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Long broken period, high merge conflict rate, impossible to bisect failures. Apply the strangler pattern instead.

failure-mode.description: Refactoring without tests — no safety net
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without tests you cannot verify behavior is preserved. The risk of introducing bugs equals a rewrite, not a refactor.

failure-mode.description: Mixing refactoring with behavior changes in the same commit
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mixed commits violate the refactoring definition (behavior unchanged) and make bisect useless.

failure-mode.description: Over-engineering: converting a 4-branch if into a 4-class strategy hierarchy
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Polymorphism adds indirection cost. Apply only when branches > 4 and each branch is non-trivial and will grow independently.

failure-mode.description: Incomplete refactoring — leaving the code in a worse intermediate state
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Half-extracted methods are harder to understand than the original. Commit only when the refactor is complete.

failure-mode.description: Agent changes types during refactoring (e.g. int to Decimal) claiming it's "cleanup"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Type changes alter behavior (rounding, comparison, serialization). Forbid type changes inside refactor commits.

failure-mode.description: Agent renames a symbol but misses string-based references (JSON keys, DB column names, reflection)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Text rename is incomplete. Require a grep of the full repo for the old name before claiming done.

failure-mode.description: Agent modifies assertion contents inside tests during a refactor commit
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Changing assertions during refactoring removes the safety net retroactively. Tests must not change except to add characterization tests for uncovered branches.
